summary: The public marketing site is live on roofingrecyclers.com with core stakeholder pages, Supabase-backed lead capture, centralized content, and an expanded RAS/capital-partner narrative. Recent work focused on rebuilding the home-page hero visual, initializing the durable project memory system, and adding optional SMTP-based lead notification emails on top of the existing Supabase capture flow.

active_workstreams.name: Marketing site polish
active_workstreams.notes: Home page and high-visibility sections are being refined to meet the intended investor-grade standard.

active_workstreams.name: Investor / capital partner narrative
active_workstreams.notes: Capital Partners page now includes RAS, business-plan, regulatory, and relationship framing.

active_workstreams.name: Lead capture and infrastructure
active_workstreams.notes: Supabase-backed form flow is implemented and deployed; SMTP notification support is now coded but still needs credentials for live delivery.

active_workstreams.name: Project memory system
active_workstreams.notes: Initialized in docs/project-memory and versioned in git on 2026-03-24.

current_priorities: Use docs/project-memory as the first-read source of truth in future sessions.
Configure SMTP credentials so lead notifications can actually send to warephoto@yahoo.com.
Validate the rebuilt home-page hero and continue visual QA on other high-visibility sections.
Plan the next investor-funnel and authority-content features.
risks: Visual quality still depends on human review across breakpoints; the rebuilt home-page hero still needs user validation after deployment.
Some future-facing metrics and impact statements are still placeholders until live data is connected.
Public-facing policy and relationship copy must remain careful to avoid overclaiming.
Lead emails will not deliver until SMTP credentials are configured in local env and Vercel.
assumptions: docs/project-memory is the canonical durable memory location for this repo.
roofingrecyclers.com remains the preferred canonical host, with www redirected.
The user wants this repo to remain clearly separate from unrelated ventures.
known_issues: Standalone npm run typecheck can fail if executed before a build because tsconfig includes .next/types output; run build first or rerun typecheck after build.
The rebuilt home-page hero passed build and typecheck locally, but the user still needs to validate the visual result in production.
Lead notifications now exist in code, but they are intentionally best-effort and currently skip sending unless SMTP environment variables are present.

recent_changes.summary: Added RAS and capital partner content, deck-based photos, and stronger investor/business-plan framing.
Aligned site metadata and schema descriptions with RAS positioning.
Initialized docs/project-memory and recorded durable project state, decisions, tasks, file index, and knowledge.
Committed and pushed the project memory system so durable knowledge is versioned in the repo and remote backup.

Rebuilt the home hero visual into a simpler full-width media block, thesis cards, and stacked operating sequence after the user flagged the prior text layout as unacceptable; local build and typecheck both passed.
Committed and pushed the hero rebuild batch to origin/main so the live site can redeploy from GitHub.

Added optional SMTP-based lead notification emails that trigger after the existing Supabase lead insert, defaulting the recipient to warephoto@yahoo.com when mailer configuration is present; build and typecheck passed.

next_steps: Add SMTP credentials locally and in Vercel so live lead emails can be delivered.
Validate the rebuilt live home-page hero and overall responsive layout with the user after deployment finishes.
Decide whether the next feature batch should focus on investor funnel improvements, authority content, or data/impact tooling.
Keep project memory updated at the end of each future session and commit memory changes when appropriate.
